title: Rial Crashes to Record 1.5 Million per Dollar
detail: The Iranian rial plunges to a new all-time low of 1.5 million per US dollar on the black market, a 9% drop in 72 hours. The Central Bank's stabilization attempt at 1.38 million has completely failed. Import costs for basic goods skyrocket, with bread prices doubling in some regions. Economists warn of hyperinflationary spiral.

media.caption: Iranian rial crashes to record 1.5 million per dollar on the black market

title: Sit-In at Tehran Grand Bazaar Dispersed with Tear Gas
detail: Hundreds of merchants and shopkeepers stage a sit-in at Tehran's Grand Bazaar, the commercial heart of the capital, demanding economic reforms and an end to the crackdown. Security forces fire tear gas and rubber bullets to disperse the crowd. The bazaar, a traditional power base for the regime, has been closed for over a week due to strikes and protests.

media.caption: Sit-in at Tehran Grand Bazaar dispersed with tear gas as merchant strikes continue

title: Reports: Iraqi Shia Militias Sent to Aid Crackdown
detail: Multiple reports indicate approximately 800 fighters from Iraqi Shia militia groups, including Kata'ib Hezbollah and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq, have crossed into Iran to assist with protest suppression. The fighters are reportedly deployed to Khuzestan and border areas. Iraq's government denies the reports. Iran's IRGC dismisses the claims as 'baseless propaganda.'

media.caption: Reports of Iraqi Shia militias deployed to assist Iran's crackdown on protests
